questions.q: Ajatashatru was the son and successor of which Magadhan king?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru was the son and successor of King Bimbisara of Magadha.

questions.q: Ajatashatru ruled the kingdom of Magadha in roughly which century BCE?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru reigned over Magadha in the 5th century BCE.

questions.q: Ajatashatru belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Like his father Bimbisara, Ajatashatru was a king of the Haryanka dynasty.

questions.q: Ajatashatru is best remembered for defeating which confederacy?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru conquered the Vajji (Lichchhavi) confederacy after a long war.

questions.q: The Vajji confederacy defeated by Ajatashatru was centred on which city?
questions.explanation: Vaishali was the chief city of the Vajji confederacy that Ajatashatru subdued.

questions.q: Ajatashatru fortified a village that later grew into which great city?
questions.explanation: He fortified Pataligrama, which grew into the celebrated city of Pataliputra.

questions.q: What was the early name of the settlement Ajatashatru fortified on the Ganges?
questions.explanation: The village was called Pataligrama before it became the city of Pataliputra.

questions.q: Ajatashatru's capital for most of his reign was which city?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru ruled mainly from Rajagriha, the established Magadhan capital.

questions.q: Ajatashatru was a contemporary of which religious teacher whose later life he is linked to?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru was a contemporary of the Buddha, whose final years fell in his reign.

questions.q: Compared to his father, Ajatashatru is remembered chiefly as a king of what kind?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru is remembered above all as an aggressive military conqueror.

questions.q: According to Buddhist tradition, how did Ajatashatru come to the throne?
options: By imprisoning and killing his father Bimbisara
By being chosen by a council of monks
questions.explanation: Buddhist sources say Ajatashatru seized power by imprisoning his father Bimbisara, who died in captivity.

questions.q: Ajatashatru's war against the Vajjis was partly aided by sowing discord among the confederacy through which minister?
questions.explanation: His minister Vassakara is said to have used diplomacy to divide the Lichchhavis of Vaishali.

questions.q: Ajatashatru is credited with using which new war engine that hurled stones against the Vajjis?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Ajatashatru with the mahashilakantaka, a stone-throwing engine used in the Vajji war.

questions.q: Ajatashatru is also said to have employed a covered chariot with swinging blades. What was it called?
questions.explanation: The rathamusala, a chariot fitted with a mace or blades, is attributed to Ajatashatru's campaigns.

questions.q: Besides the Vajjis, Ajatashatru also fought a long conflict with which neighbouring kingdom?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru fought a prolonged conflict with Kosala under King Prasenajit.

questions.q: After the Buddha's death, Ajatashatru is said to have received a share of what?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru is traditionally said to have obtained a share of the Buddha's relics and enshrined them.

questions.q: The First Buddhist Council, convened soon after the Buddha's death, was held under whose patronage?
questions.explanation: The First Buddhist Council at Rajagriha was held under the patronage of Ajatashatru.

questions.q: Why did Ajatashatru fortify Pataligrama on the Ganges?
options: As a coastal trading port for Rome
As a strategic base against the Vajjis
As a summer retreat in the hills
questions.explanation: He fortified Pataligrama as a forward military base for his campaign against the Vajjis.

questions.q: Who is traditionally said to have succeeded Ajatashatru on the Magadhan throne?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru was succeeded by his son Udayin, who is linked to making Pataliputra the capital.

questions.q: Ajatashatru's defeat of the Vajjis marked the decline of which form of governance in the region?
questions.explanation: His conquest of Vaishali hastened the eclipse of the republican gana-sangha polities by monarchy.

questions.q: Ajatashatru also receives mention in the traditions of which religion besides Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Jain tradition also records Ajatashatru, calling him Kunika, as a contemporary of Mahavira.

questions.q: Jain texts refer to Ajatashatru by which name?
questions.explanation: In Jain tradition Ajatashatru is known as Kunika, son of Shrenika (Bimbisara).

questions.q: The war with Kosala arose partly from a dispute over a village in which region, gifted as dowry to Bimbisara?
questions.explanation: The conflict centred on a Kashi village originally given to Bimbisara, which Kosala sought to reclaim.

questions.q: Which Kosalan king, Ajatashatru's maternal uncle and adversary, was deposed by his own son during the war?
questions.explanation: Prasenajit of Kosala, Ajatashatru's uncle, was overthrown by his son Virudhaka.

questions.q: Ajatashatru's mother, by some accounts a Kosalan princess, gave the war with Kosala what added dimension?
options: A family feud between related dynasties
questions.explanation: Because the royal houses were linked by marriage, the Kosala war was also a dispute among kin.

questions.q: The Vajji confederacy that Ajatashatru destroyed was a union led principally by which clan?
questions.explanation: The Lichchhavis of Vaishali were the dominant clan of the Vajji confederacy.

questions.q: Roughly how long is Ajatashatru's campaign against the Vajjis traditionally said to have lasted?
questions.explanation: Tradition describes the Vajji war as a drawn-out struggle lasting many years before Vaishali fell.

questions.q: Ajatashatru's reign is conventionally dated by many historians to approximately which span?
questions.explanation: Many traditional chronologies place Ajatashatru's reign around 492 to 460 BCE, though dates remain debated.

questions.q: The Buddhist text describing Ajatashatru visiting the Buddha and discussing the fruits of asceticism is the:
questions.explanation: The Samannaphala Sutta records Ajatashatru's visit to the Buddha and their dialogue.

questions.q: By consolidating control over the Ganges plain, Ajatashatru advanced Magadha toward becoming what?
options: The paramount power of northern India
questions.explanation: His conquests left Magadha the dominant power of the northern Indian heartland.
